dialog.stepLabel: Rover: "These Echoes... Are they guarding the vault?..."

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: These Echoes... Are they guarding the vault?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yes. But I've never seen them in action before. The "Averardo Bank Employee Manual" says they can easily take out a whole squad of Resonators.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Of course, this doesn't apply to someone powerful like you, {PlayerName}. Still, their combat skills are formidable. Their mere presence is enough to make everyone feel safe.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: This facility houses nearly half of Rinascita's wealth. It serves all of Ragunna's citizens, even including the Fisalias.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Despite our feud, no one in Ragunna can refuse the services of Averardo Vault.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What the... I thought they only responded to hostility!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Steady now. Don't move. It's on high alert but won't attack unless provoked. At least, that's what the Employee Manual says!
Stand down.
I apologize for the small mishap on behalf of the Montelli Family. And...
What a pleasant surprise to see you again so soon.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: And you.
Yet your expression betrays no surprise, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Allow me to properly introduce myself. My name is Carlotta, Carlotta Montelli.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Montelli… You are the…

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The second daughter of the Montelli Family, yes. Please feel free to call me Carlotta.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Also, I trust the renumeration meets your satisfaction, Ms. Zani?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It was you who tasked me with receiving {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Surely a distinguished guest such as {PlayerName} deserves the attention of our most trusted employee.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I'm aware of your purpose here. On behalf of my family, allow me to extend our deepest gratitude for your endeavors.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Your eyes show curiosity about me and my role in this… drama. However, this isn't the place for such discussions. Please follow me.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Make it quick and clean.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Copy that.

dialog.stepLabel: Carlotta: "Ms. Zani, do you mind handling the cleanup? I'm sorry. I jus..."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Ms. Zani, do you mind handling the cleanup? I'm sorry. I just promised you a vacation... but looks like it will have to wait.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Oh it's fine. Just a bit of extra work. I can't stand by and watch my colleagues getting hurt.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Besides, you have bigger things to worry about. Just let me handle this.

options.text: We can always count on you, Zani.
Be sure to get that overtime pay.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yeah, yeah… That's the burden of being good at my job.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: There's more to this attack than it seems.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The Order must have been behind it, right?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Yes. At this juncture, neither the Fractsidus nor the Fisalias have any reason to interfere. It's some hidden truth about the Order the Sentinel is leading us towards.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The traitors must be watching us, knowing truth is on the verge of revelation.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I have a feeling that whatever's coming next… will not be easy.

dialog.stepLabel: Carlotta: "This is where my family first found the Vitreum Dancer...."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: This is where my family first found the Vitreum Dancer.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: What do you see in the shattered stained glass?

options.text: It seems to be telling a story.
Is there a way to piece them together?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: This seems to chronicle a legend of the Sentinel and the tale of how the first Primus founded the Order.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I've learned of this history before but many details have faded over time.

options.text: How do you know all this?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Well, art investment is one of our family's key ventures, so I've naturally picked up a thing or two along the way.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: There is a stone tablet over there. It might offer some clues about these stained glass images.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It's done. Now let's see…

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Looks like these stained glass images recount a tale about the Divinity. Wait...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: What is it?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It's just... the content is rather peculiar. I'll translate it for you.

dialog.stepLabel: Carlotta: "Rinascita has two divinities? I've never heard of such a thi..."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Rinascita has two divinities? I've never heard of such a thing.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: The Montelli family archives hold many documents on Rinascita's past, yet none mentioned two divinities.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: In Ragunna, the common belief has always been that Sentinel Imperator is the only divinity of Rinascita.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: But if the images on the stained glass windows were fabricated, why would the Order deface them and declare this tower forbidden?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: There's only one explanation. These are not fabrications but buried truth.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: If this is indeed the Order's doing... we must leave here immediately.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: North of this tower is an island.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: It should be beyond their reach. Once we get there, you will discover another truth the Order has buried—

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Watch out!

options.text: They surely know when to show up.
You go first. I'll cover you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: …Interesting, they're here even faster than I anticipated.

dialog.stepLabel: Carlotta: "Keep it safe. I'll handle the rest...."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Keep it safe. I'll handle the rest.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Speak, who gave you the order?

dialog.stepLabel: Carlotta: "Look at what you have done, distorting history, usurping the..."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Look at what you have done, distorting history, usurping the Sentinel's authority, and... colluding with wretches.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Who are your accomplices? The Fractsidus, the Fisalias, and even some of the Montellis, am I right?

items.speaker: Order Envoy
items.text: Ahem… I'm afraid I don't understand, Miss Carlotta. It seems you too are lost to madness and hallucination…

items.speaker: Order Envoy
items.text: If you find yourself confused and desire the Sentinel's guidance, I would gladly arrange... a personal Pilgrim's Sail for you.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: So this is how you treat those truth-seekers? Banish them in the name of pilgrimage?
—Please, lower your weapon. There is still room for discussion, is there not, Miss Carlotta?

dialog.stepLabel: Priest Alessio: "Stand down. Enough blood has been shed today. Imperator woul..."

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Stand down. Enough blood has been shed today. Imperator would not revel in senseless sacrifice.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: And the Pilgrim's Sail is for the transgressors to seek rebirth, not for you to threaten our friends. Repent for your reckless words.

items.speaker: Order Envoy
items.text: …Forgive me, Brother Alessio.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Ah, honored guest. You have been misinformed, as the truth is not what it seems. My lord Fenrico has arranged your audience with the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Why not hear the Sentinel's wisdom for yourself?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Of course, if Lord Fenrico is the true "Enlightened One."

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: However, the prophecy speaks of a maiden as the "Enlightened One." Is that why you destroyed the stained glass? To pass Fenrico off as the Enlightened One? So he can seize the Sentinel's power and rule Ragunna?

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Oh, you stubborn people of little faith. You've surely been tempted by evil and swallowed its lies. Lord Fenrico is the undeniable Enlightened One and will guide us all.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Well, I see no point in debating doctrine with you. Let's move on to a more concrete subject. Now, Miss Carlotta, which one do you think is more important to the Montelli family?

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Is it "Profit"…

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: …or "Bloodline"?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: How… that's… Uncle Capollo's pendant? What did you do to him!

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Good question. What did we do indeed? Quite a faithful one he was.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Wouldn't hand over this pendant, the emblem of his faith... but we managed to persuade him.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Whether he should be deemed faithful now depends entirely on your actions. And you know what will become of him if we decide his devotion is weak and unworthy.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: …My actions? What do you want me to do?

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Raise your weapon, faithful Carlotta. Choose wisely now… Where shall you aim? Whose heart will taste your bullet?

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Good. You are a smart one, Miss Carlotta.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: …Carlotta?

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: I'm sorry. Even though the time we shared was… very memorable.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Excellent. It appears you've made the right choice as any true Ragunnesi would, honoring blood and faith alike.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Pull the trigger. End this transgressor's life. The Sentinel shall host a supreme banquet for you in the high heavens.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: Farewell, and may you meet your friends… where it all ends.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: …You've disappointed me, Miss Carlotta.

items.speaker: Carlotta
items.text: There is a saying in Ragunna, even the finest bullet can miss. It was only a mistake.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: So be it. I shouldn't question your faith over such a mishap... It is after all insignificant in the grand scheme of the Divinity.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Let us bear witness, to the moment divine wrath descends from the heavens.

dialog.stepLabel: Zani: "Averardo Vault is just up ahead. It seems this is where we p..."

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Averardo Vault is just up ahead. It seems this is where we part ways, Noah.

items.speaker: Noah
items.text: *A cry of understanding*

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'm sure it can find its way back to the harbor. Don't worry. Now, we just need to follow this path to reach the vault. Let's go.
